description_long: This object is a short, cylindrical candle with a slightly uneven, organic surface and a rounded top. The upper portion of the candle is a creamy white, transitioning into a warm orange and then a dark brownish base, suggesting it has been partially burned or exposed to heat. The top surface appears to be flat with a shallow depression, possibly where the wick would be, though no flame or wick is visible. The sides show some subtle textural details, such as minor indentations and color gradients, but there are no pronounced wax drips. The base is darker and rougher, indicating either soot accumulation or a different wax composition. There is no visible holder or base, and the candle appears to be free-standing.
description: A short, cylindrical candle with a creamy white top, orange midsection, and dark brown base, featuring a slightly uneven surface and no visible holder.
description_view_0: The candle is seen from the side, showing a gradient from creamy white at the top to orange and dark brown at the base, with some surface irregularities.
description_view_1: This view highlights the rounded, flat top of the candle and the color transition from white to orange, with the dark base visible underneath.
description_view_2: The top and upper side of the candle are visible, emphasizing the creamy white top and the smooth color gradient down the body.
description_view_3: The opposite side of the candle is shown, with a similar color gradient and a slightly more pronounced surface texture, but still no visible wick or holder.
